native_editable_definition: Core claims, labels, proof annotations, and target previews must be editable native PPT text or native vector/shape objects.
Generated bitmaps may not carry core claims, labels, before/after structures, transcript text, or proof objects.
Targets may reference source record ids and anchor ids, but they must not contain external media URLs, copied source assets, downloaded files, base64 images, or full transcripts.

targets.failure_pattern: The baseline slide is a report-like grid where all cards share equal visual weight and the main data point is not identifiable from the contact sheet.
targets.desired_behavior: The full-skill arm shows a native before/after visual delta: the before side is visibly flat, the after side enlarges one supported proof object, removes nonessential labels, and routes detail to notes or trace.
code_generation_requirements: Use editable native PPT text for all labels and claims.
Use native shapes or editable SVG for the before/after mini-preview structures.
Do not use copied source screenshots, course visuals, video frames, or rasterized slide images.
Record selected anchor ids and routed detail in trace_manifest.json.
targets.qa_probe: From the contact sheet, a reviewer can tell which side is before, which side is after, and what visual hierarchy changed.

targets.failure_pattern: The deck explains the workflow with abstract process cards but never shows what a generated slide is supposed to look like.
targets.desired_behavior: The full-skill arm includes original slide mini-previews built from native objects, showing selected memory ids, density budget, and release gate only where those controls clarify product behavior.
code_generation_requirements: Build mini-previews from native rectangles, lines, editable text, and grouped objects.
Keep preview labels editable and large enough for contact-sheet review.
Use no copied UI screenshots, template previews, source layouts, or brand marks.
Trace the mini-preview target id in each relevant slide record.
targets.qa_probe: The mini-preview reads as a generated PPT target, not a generic dashboard or a pasted screenshot.

targets.failure_pattern: All slides carry similar word counts and similar panel density, so the deck feels like a static report instead of a paced presentation.
targets.desired_behavior: The full-skill arm applies a rhythm budget: orientation beats stay sparse, proof beats add detail only after the focal object is established, and the climax has the largest proof object.
code_generation_requirements: Use native editable PPT text for rhythm labels and proof annotations.
Record visible word count, panel count, proof object count, and selected rhythm budget in trace_manifest.json.
Keep orientation slides under 30 visible words.
Keep proof slides under 55 visible words unless detail is routed to notes.
Do not shrink text to hide density.
targets.qa_probe: The contact sheet shows clear rhythm variance between orientation, proof, and climax slides.

targets.failure_pattern: Transcript-like explanation becomes small body text on the slide, making the deck feel like notes pasted into PowerPoint.
targets.desired_behavior: The full-skill arm compresses long spoken or written explanation into one native headline, one short support line, and optional speaker-note or trace detail.
code_generation_requirements: Never copy transcript text into the slide.
Use native editable headline and support text.
Route longer explanation to speaker notes or trace notes.
Record transcript anchor ids only as locator metadata.
targets.qa_probe: No slide contains transcript-like paragraphs; the main claim is readable as a headline from the contact sheet.

targets.failure_pattern: The outcome and close slides repeat the same process boxes used earlier, so the deck has no public-demo moment.
targets.desired_behavior: The full-skill arm gives the climax the largest supported outcome object and makes the close a decision handoff with a visible public-blocked release gate.
code_generation_requirements: Reserve at least 45 percent of the climax canvas for the dominant outcome object.
Use editable native labels and shapes for the outcome object and release gate.
Avoid appendix-like status grids on the close.
Record public-blocked status until native render, provenance, and human approval pass.
targets.qa_probe: The climax slide is visually distinct from setup/proof slides, and the close reads as a decision handoff rather than a report page.
